Lamination Adhesive System: ECOLAD SB-940/ECOLAD SB-527
DESCRIPTION & APPLICATIONS:
ECOLAD SB-940 co-reacted with ECOLAD SB-527 is a solvent-based, two-component polyurethane laminating adhesive
for flexible packaging. The cross–linked adhesive film has good bond strength, transparency, flexibility and antiaging
performance. The cured adhesive film is odorless. The system has low viscosity and can be applied at high-solid content.
It has good initial tack, and excellent mechanical and wetting properties.
TYPICAL USES:
ECOLAD SB-940/ECOLAD SB-527 is a medium performance polyurethane laminating adhesive suitable for a variety of
substrates such as pre –treated PE film, PP film, PET film, PA film, metalized film, film-foil laminates. PE and PP films should
be corona treated. PE films should be above 40 dynes/cm. PP films should be above 38 dynes/cm.
SPECIFICATIONS:
PROPERTIES ECOLAD SB-940 ECOLAD SB-527
Functionality OH NCO
Color/Appearance Yellow/Clear Yellow/Clear
Solid Content/Active
component [%] 72 ± 2% 75 ± 2%
Viscosity at 25⁰C [mPa.s] 6500 ± 1000 1800 ± 600
Solvent Ethyl Acetate Ethyl Acetate
Mixing Ratio p.b.w 21 3.5
COMPLIANCE WITH FOOD PACKAGING REGULATIONS:
ECOLAD SB-940/ECOLAD SB-527 is in compositional compliance with US FDA 21 CFR 175.105 and (EU) 2023/1627 of 10
August 2023, amending Regulation (EU) No 10/2011. Under specific conditions of the FDA, EC and BFR regulations, the
fully cured adhesive is suitable for the lamination of food packaging materials. A conformity letter with further details is
available on request. The responsibility for compliance with the specific regulations is with the converter and not with the
adhesive supplier
DILUTION:
Suitable solvents are ethyl acetate, acetone and methyl ethyl ketone (MEK) with a water content not exceeding 300
ppm. Aromatic and alcoholic solvents are not suitable
MIXING PROCEDURE:
ECOLAD SB-940 must be diluted with the corresponding solvent to the desired solid content before application. Then the
correct amount of ECOLAD SB-527 must be added under agitation. The preferred solvent here is ethyl acetate.
Solid Content (%) ECOLAD SB-940
(Kg)
ECOLAD SB-527
(Kg)
ETHYL ACETATE
(Kg)
VISCOSITY, sec (25°C)
FORD CUP # 4
50 6 1 3.26 40 – 42
45 6 1 4.40 26 – 28
40 6 1 5.83 20 – 22
35 6 1 7.66 16 – 18
30 6 1 10.10 14 –15

-- 1 of 3 --

Page 2 of 3
A partner for life.
www.bciholding.com
COATING WEIGHT:
Depending on the film or foil combination and the end use of the laminate, the amount of adhesive applied must range
from 2-4 g/m2. Laminated films and foils for further processing may require higher weight. For printed films, the weight
must be adjusted.
CURING:
Storing of the laminates in a hot room is necessary to shorten the time for the laminates to be rewound and slit. When
stored in a hot room, the curing time for rewinding & slitting is 24 - 48 hours at 45oC – 50oC and at 50 – 60% relative
humidity. When stored at 25oC – 30oC, the curing time for rewinding and slitting is 72 – 90 hours at 50 – 60% relative
humidity. The complete cross-linking/curing at 45oC - 50oC occurs within 5 – 6 days at 50 – 60% relative humidity. At
ambient temperature of 25oC – 30oC, complete cross-linking/curing occurs within 7-12 days. Manufacture of triplex
laminates is 24 hours after the production of duplex laminates, duplex laminates being stored in a hot room at 45oC –
50oC and at 50 – 60% relative humidity. The triplex laminates need a curing time of 24 - 48 hours at 45oC – 50oC and at
50 – 60% relative humidity for it to be rewound and slit. As the rate of curing is also dependent on temperature, this
means that storage at elevated temperature will reduce the curing time.
Total resistance to severe environments will only be achieved after complete curing and this usually takes 7 - 12 days of
storage at 25oC – 30oC depending on film combination and intended use of the laminates. When stored in a hot room
(45oC – 50oC), the complete curing can be achieved within 4 – 6 days.
POT LIFE:
The ready-to-use adhesive ECOLAD SB-940/ECOLAD SB-527 (30%-38%) in closed containers has a working time of
approximately 8 hours without substantial increase in viscosity. Overage adhesive mixtures and old adhesive in the glue
pot affect adversely the quality of the laminates.
STORAGE:
ECOLAD SB-940 and ECOLAD SB-527 are highly flammable because of the presence of ethyl acetate. Store all materials
in dry, clear space that is adequately ventilated with temperature not exceeding 30°C and away from any source of
spark/ignition.
ECOLAD SB-940 and ECOLAD SB-527 can be kept unopened for 12 months at temperatures between 10°C and 30°C.
Partially used drums (especially ECOLAD SB-527) should be closed again tightly and should be used completely as quickly
as possible.
PACKAGING:
Film additives – particularly slip agent content in LDPE, MDPE, and CPP films – packed goods, printing inks, pre-treatment
and coating of films are of significant importance to the end use of the laminates and may affect the performance
properties either instantly or at a later time. Therefore, practical laminating tests as well as proper checks on the laminates
are necessary before going into full-scale production.
ECOLAD SB-940: 21 kg ECOLAD SB-527: 3.5 kg

Please refer to SDS for the safe handling and precautions of adhesive during production.

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
Disclaimer and other information
Processing conditions like temperature, pressure and web tensions, adhesive application and coating weight, films and their compositions (i.e.
thickness and additives used, printing inks, filling goods, surface treatment and coatings on films) are of significant importance to achieve
optimum performance of the laminate. Small trials as well as proper checks on the laminates are necessary before running full scale production
job.
The information provided in the data sheet, especially recommendations for the usage and the applications of our products, is based on our
knowledge and experience. Due to the use of various types of materials as well as varying working conditions which are beyond our control,
we strictly recommend thorough trials to test the suitability of our products with regard to the required processes and end use applications.
We do not accept any liability with regard to the above information or with regard to any verbal recommendations except in cases where we
are liable of gross negligence or false intention.
Date of Issue: September 13, 2023
